title: Velvety Bitter Uruguayan Cream Bisque
description: A silky bisque that showcases Uruguays love of bitter yerba mate over a smooth singlecream base, tempered with aromatic spices for a showoff splash of flavor.

ingredients.name: 1% single cream
ingredients.notes: creamy but low‑fat for a velvety texture

instructions: Brew the yerba mate: steep 2 tsp of dried leaves in 200 ml of hot water, let steep 5 min, then strain.
Heat the vegetable stock in a saucepan until it reaches 80 °C, then lower the heat.
In a small pan, warm the sesame oil over low heat and add cumin and coriander; toast until fragrant (about 30 s), then remove from heat.
Blend the roasted tomato puree with tomato paste until smooth, then stir it into the warm stock.
Add the tempered spice mixture to the stock and bring to a gentle simmer for 5 minutes to marry flavors.
Slowly pour in the single‑cream, whisking continuously to prevent curdling; let the bisque come to a low simmer, stirring constantly, for 10 minutes.
Incorporate the yerba mate infusion and lemon juice; simmer the bisque another 5 minutes, keeping temperature below 95 °C.
Season with sea salt; adjust seasoning as needed. Serve hot, optionally garnished with a swirl of single‑cream or a sprinkle of fresh herbs.
Optional: finish with a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil for added sheen.
